CVE-2014-3421.title: lisp/gnus/gnus-fun.el in GNU Emacs 24.3 and earlier allows local  ...
CVE-2014-3421.summary: lisp/gnus/gnus-fun.el in GNU Emacs 24.3 and earlier allows local users to overwrite arbitrary files via a symlink attack on the /tmp/gnus.face.ppm temporary file.

CVE-2022-45939.title: GNU Emacs through 28.2 allows attackers to execute commands via s ...
CVE-2022-45939.summary: GNU Emacs through 28.2 allows attackers to execute commands via shell metacharacters in the name of a source-code file, because lib-src/etags.c uses the system C library function in its implementation of the ctags program. For example, a victim may use the "ctags *" command (suggested in the ctags documentation) in a situation where the current working directory has contents that depend on untrusted input.

CVE-2022-48337.title: GNU Emacs through 28.2 allows attackers to execute commands via s ...
CVE-2022-48337.summary: GNU Emacs through 28.2 allows attackers to execute commands via shell metacharacters in the name of a source-code file, because lib-src/etags.c uses the system C library function in its implementation of the etags program. For example, a victim may use the "etags -u *" command (suggested in the etags documentation) in a situation where the current working directory has contents that depend on untrusted input.

CVE-2022-48338.title: An issue was discovered in GNU Emacs through 28.2. In ruby-mode.e ...
CVE-2022-48338.summary: An issue was discovered in GNU Emacs through 28.2. In ruby-mode.el, the ruby-find-library-file function has a local command injection vulnerability. The ruby-find-library-file function is an interactive function, and bound to C-c C-f. Inside the function, the external command gem is called through shell-command-to-string, but the feature-name parameters are not escaped. Thus, malicious Ruby source files may cause commands to be executed.

CVE-2022-48339.title: An issue was discovered in GNU Emacs through 28.2. htmlfontify.el ...
CVE-2022-48339.summary: An issue was discovered in GNU Emacs through 28.2. htmlfontify.el has a command injection vulnerability. In the hfy-istext-command function, the parameter file and parameter srcdir come from external input, and parameters are not escaped. If a file name or directory name contains shell metacharacters, code may be executed.

CVE-2023-27985.title: emacsclient-mail.desktop in Emacs 28.1 through 28.2 is vulnerable ...
CVE-2023-27985.summary: emacsclient-mail.desktop in Emacs 28.1 through 28.2 is vulnerable to shell command injections through a crafted mailto: URI. This is related to lack of compliance with the Desktop Entry Specification. It is fixed in 29.0.90

CVE-2023-27986.title: emacsclient-mail.desktop in Emacs 28.1 through 28.2 is vulnerable ...
CVE-2023-27986.summary: emacsclient-mail.desktop in Emacs 28.1 through 28.2 is vulnerable to Emacs Lisp code injections through a crafted mailto: URI with unescaped double-quote characters. It is fixed in 29.0.90.

CVE-2023-28617.title: org-babel-execute:latex in ob-latex.el in Org Mode through 9.6.1  ...
CVE-2023-28617.summary: org-babel-execute:latex in ob-latex.el in Org Mode through 9.6.1 for GNU Emacs allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a file name or directory name that contains shell metacharacters.

CVE-2024-30202.title: In Emacs before 29.3, arbitrary Lisp code is evaluated as part of ...
CVE-2024-30202.summary: In Emacs before 29.3, arbitrary Lisp code is evaluated as part of turning on Org mode. This affects Org Mode before 9.6.23.

CVE-2024-30203.title: In Emacs before 29.3, Gnus treats inline MIME contents as trusted ...
CVE-2024-30203.summary: In Emacs before 29.3, Gnus treats inline MIME contents as trusted.

CVE-2024-30204.title: In Emacs before 29.3, LaTeX preview is enabled by default for e-m ...
CVE-2024-30204.summary: In Emacs before 29.3, LaTeX preview is enabled by default for e-mail attachments.

CVE-2024-30205.title: In Emacs before 29.3, Org mode considers contents of remote files ...
CVE-2024-30205.summary: In Emacs before 29.3, Org mode considers contents of remote files to be trusted. This affects Org Mode before 9.6.23.

CVE-2024-39331.title: In Emacs before 29.4, org-link-expand-abbrev in lisp/ol.el expand ...
CVE-2024-39331.summary: In Emacs before 29.4, org-link-expand-abbrev in lisp/ol.el expands a %(...) link abbrev even when it specifies an unsafe function, such as shell-command-to-string. This affects Org Mode before 9.7.5.

CVE-2024-53920.title: In elisp-mode.el in GNU Emacs before 30.1, a user who chooses to  ...
CVE-2024-53920.summary: In elisp-mode.el in GNU Emacs before 30.1, a user who chooses to invoke elisp-completion-at-point (for code completion) on untrusted Emacs Lisp source code can trigger unsafe Lisp macro expansion that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code. (This unsafe expansion also occurs if a user chooses to enable on-the-fly diagnosis that byte compiles untrusted Emacs Lisp source code.)

CVE-2025-1244.title: A command injection flaw was found in the text editor Emacs. It c ...
CVE-2025-1244.summary: A command injection flaw was found in the text editor Emacs. It could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary shell commands on a vulnerable system. Exploitation is possible by tricking users into visiting a specially crafted website or an HTTP URL with a redirect.
